D10 Imaging L5 Evaluate Reference

ColorMatrix vs ForwardMatrix

DNG ships two matrices per illuminant — ColorMatrix goes XYZ→camera (white-balance fitting); ForwardMatrix goes camera→XYZ (rendering). They are not inverses.

A DNG profile stores both a ColorMatrix (XYZ→camera, used to find the illuminant by fitting the as-shot neutral) and a ForwardMatrix (camera→XYZ-D50, used to render the image to the PCS). They are fit under different constraints and are not inverses of each other — they are stored together precisely because they answer different questions.

Pick a camera-native RGB. Path A: invert ColorMatrix to get XYZ; Path B: apply ForwardMatrix to get XYZ. Both targets should describe the same physical colour — but the matrices were fit to different data, so the two estimates do not agree exactly.
